question_text: A ball is dropped from a height and falls towards the ground. Which energy transformation occurs?

options.label: A
options.text: Kinetic energy transforms to potential energy

options.label: B
options.text: Potential energy transforms to kinetic energy

options.label: C
options.text: Thermal energy transforms to kinetic energy

options.label: D
options.text: Chemical energy transforms to potential energy

correct_answer: Potential energy transforms to kinetic energy
explanation: When a ball is dropped, it starts with gravitational potential energy (due to its height). As it falls, this potential energy decreases while kinetic energy increases. The total mechanical energy stays the same because energy is conserved - it just changes from one form to another.
